title: Top Five Stephen King Movies and the Most Anticipated Movies at the Toronto International Film Festival
format: Sean Fennessey and Adam Nayman draft dueling top fives of Stephen King movie adaptations ahead of 'It Chapter Two,' plus a preview of what Adam's watching for at TIFF.

segments.heading: TIFF preview, from Telluride and Venice buzz

films.title: Waves
films.note: Trey Edward Shults's film. Adam's Telluride writeup was persuasive enough that Sean rearranged his TIFF schedule around it.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: The Safdies' follow-up. Word out of Telluride was that it played 'electric.'

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: Noah Baumbach's film, on Adam's must-see list.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Bong Joon Ho's Palme d'Or winner. Six hundred people got turned away from a 9 a.m. Telluride screening; it had already made about $85 million in South Korea.

films.title: Joker
films.note: Discussed mostly as a punchline about festival-season hyperbole, not as a film either has seen yet.

films.title: Anne at 13,000 Ft.
films.note: Kazik Radwanski's film, starring Deragh Campbell. Adam stumping for it as an under-the-radar TIFF pick. It premiered at this festival in 2019 but didn't get a wide release until 2021.

films.title: White Lie
films.note: Yonah Lewis and Calvin Thomas's film, starring Kacey Rohl. Adam's other under-the-radar TIFF pick.

films.title: Roma
films.note: Raised as the closest parallel to what Bong's doing with Parasite: a filmmaker returning to make a movie fully inside his own culture.

films.title: Snowpiercer
films.note: Cited as proof Bong's English-language films already play as credible hits before Parasite.

films.title: Okja
films.note: Same point as Snowpiercer, above.

segments.heading: Adam Nayman's top five
segments.note: best to worst; his No. 5 was a tie
segments.subject: Stephen King movie adaptations

films.title: The Shining
films.note: No. 1, shared with Sean. The movie Adam's watched the most times in his life; he credits 'Room 237' as the best film ever made about it.

films.title: Carrie
films.note: No. 2. His favorite De Palma movie for the performances. Sissy Spacek's work, in his words, is what makes the ending's revenge so cathartic.

films.title: The Dead Zone
films.note: No. 3. Cronenberg's, built on Christopher Walken right before he tipped into self-parody.

films.title: Christine
films.note: No. 4. The one with the least consensus behind it on his list. He likes it as an inverted 'Revenge of the Nerds,' anti-American-Graffiti movie.

films.title: The Mist
films.note: No. 5, tied with Shawshank. Praises Darabont for rewriting the ending and actually following through on the apocalyptic dread instead of pulling back.

films.title: The Shawshank Redemption
films.note: No. 5, tied with The Mist. Sees it and The Mist as the two poles of King on screen (one redemptive, one bleak) from the same director.

segments.heading: Sean Fennessy's top five
segments.note: best to worst; his No. 5 was also a tie
segments.subject: Stephen King movie adaptations

films.title: The Shining
films.note: No. 1, shared with Adam. Can't think of ten movies that mean more to him personally.

films.title: Stand By Me
films.note: No. 2. Saw it around the age of the kids in the film; admits on a rewatch it dragged a little, but the sense-memory of it as a kid stuck.

films.title: Carrie
films.note: No. 3. Also Adam's No. 2. The two of them agree this is the standout.

films.title: Misery
films.note: No. 4. Kathy Bates's Oscar-winning performance; still the only movie based on a King novel to win an Oscar of any kind. James Caan was reportedly about the thirtieth actor offered the role, after William Hurt, Kevin Kline, Michael Douglas, Harrison Ford, Dustin Hoffman, Robert De Niro, Al Pacino, Richard Dreyfuss, Gene Hackman, and Robert Redford all passed or were passed over.

films.title: The Mist
films.note: No. 5, tied with Shawshank. Watched the black-and-white cut for the first time and thinks it plays better than the color version.

films.title: The Shawshank Redemption
films.note: No. 5, tied with The Mist. Knows the message-board backlash is coming and has made peace with it.

films.title: The Running Man
films.note: Adam's honorable mention: 'a really dumb, goofy movie' that isn't faithful to the book, but he credits it with inventing 'American Gladiators' by proxy. Richard Dawson as the host, Jesse Ventura and Toru Tanaka among the hunters.

films.title: Needful Things
films.note: Sean's pick, mostly for Max von Sydow's performance as the devilish shopkeeper: 'nihilistically bleak' in a way he likes.

films.title: In the Mouth of Madness
films.note: Adam's stump for the best King movie that isn't a King adaptation: John Carpenter's satire of King and Lovecraft, with a Sutter Kane character (Jürgen Prochnow) who 'outsells Stephen King two to one.'

segments.heading: What's next: the Stephen King boom
segments.note: with It Chapter Two days away

films.title: Doctor Sleep
films.note: Mike Flanagan's Shining sequel, days from release. Sean had been reading the novel on a flight; both wonder whether it can satisfy Shining loyalists and King readers at once.

films.title: Gerald's Game
films.note: Also Flanagan, cited as one of the better recent King adaptations.

films.title: Pet Sematary
films.note: That year's remake. Adam wrote about it for the site; Sean liked the original and didn't much care for this version, though he rates the directors talented.

films.title: Salem's Lot
films.note: Mentioned in passing as another King property getting a new film adaptation, prompting the question of whether the genre is entering more of a remake era than a new-adaptation one.

segments.heading: The worst Stephen King movie ever made

picks.title: Maximum Overdrive
picks.note: The only movie King has both written and directed. 'It's just really bad.'

picks.title: Dreamcatcher
picks.note: Written by William Goldman and Lawrence Kasdan, directed by Kasdan, shot by John Seale, and starring Morgan Freeman, Thomas Jane, Jason Lee, Damian Lewis, and Timothy Olyphant; and still, somehow, 'a piece of shit.'
